Practices Commission

city Attorney
P. O. Box 130
Antioch, CA 94509-0504
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-92-068
Dear Mr. Galstan:
This Letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the responsibi l ities of Councllmember Barbara Price
under the conf l- ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political 
Reform
Act (the rra6grr1.1 our advice is prospective in nature. we make
no comments regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A),)
OUESTIONS
1. Do the conf t ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act require
Councj-lrnember Price to disqualify herself from participating in
governmental decisions regarding the reorganization of the
Riverview Fire Protection District?
2. Does the rrpublic generally" exception appl.y to enable
Council-member Price to participate in such decisions?
CONCLUS TONS
1. The conf fj.ct-of- interest provi.s j.ons of the Act require
Councilmember Price to disqualify herself frorn participating in
governmentaf decisions regarding the reorganization of the
Riverview Fire Protection Di.strict if the decisions wilL have a

regulations appear at 2 cal-ifornia code of Regulations sectj-on
18OOO, et seg. All references to regulations are to Title 2,

reasonably foreseeable material financiat effect on her econornic
interests .
2. The itpublic generally" exception does not apply to the
facts you have presented on behalf of councilmember Price.
FACTS
The Riverview Fire Protection District (the t'districtr'), 
a
district 
organized pursuant to the Fire Protection District 
Lae, of
1,961, Healtt C Satety Code Sections 1380L-13999, Provides fire
protection services to the City of Antioch. The district 
also
provides services to the City of Pittsburg and to the
unincorporated communj,ty of west Pittsburg-
The district 
is governed by the contra costa county Board of
Supervisors. A locaL appointed fire commission rnakes
re^cornmendations to the Board of Supervisors. The City of Antioch
is considering a number of ideas about the district's 
future
organization. These include proposals to remove the Board of
Supervisors as the governing body of the distri'ct and to replace
it with a loca1ly-elected fire board. Another proposal is to
dissolve the dislrict, 
thereby creating a new f j-re departnent
within the city of Antioch.
council-member Price's spouse is emptoyed by the district 
as a
firefighter. 
Mr' Price is also the president of the district's
tirefi{nters' 
union and receives an annual salary or stipend for
this oifice which is in excess of $250. This salary or stipend is
paid frorn mernbership dues. You have advised rne that it is your
Lelief that the union is a nonprofit corporation'
You are the city attorney for the city of Antioch' .In that
capacity, and with the authorization of Councilmernber Prj-ce, you
="LX orri'advice to determine whether the provis5-ons of the Act
require councilmember Price to disqualify herself from
paiticipating in decisions regarding the district.
ANALYSTS
Secti-on 871o0 prohibits public offj'ciaIs from making,
participating in, or using their official 
position to inf lu-ence a
-governn^ental. decision in which they know or have reason to know
fhey have a financial interest. 
Councilmember Price is a public
off i-cial . 
(Section 82048 . )
Financial- Interests

rneaningofsectionsTlooifitisreasonab}yforeseeable.thatthe
decj,si5n will- have a material financial effect, distinguishable

Page 3
from its effect on the pubJ,ic generalty, on the official, 
or on a
member of the official's 
inmediate fanily,z or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a conmercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregati,ng two hundred fifty
dollars (9250) or more in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the tine when the
decision is rnade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
empl,oyee, or holds any position of management.
Section 87103 (c) and (d) .
A public official's 
economic interests include any community
property interest in the income of a spouse. (Section 82030(a).
accorilingly, Councj,Imember Price nust disqualify herself frorn
participiting 
in governnental decisions which will foreseeabl-y and
inaterially aifect her spouse's sources of income of $509 or more
in the twelve months prlceding a governmental' decision.3
You have stated in your request for advice that councilmember
Price's spouse is a firefighter 
and receives income frorn the
Riverview Fire Protection District, a local government entity' 
As
you correctly point out, this source of income is not a
disqualifying financial- j,nterest. For purposes of the Act,
r,inCome" does not inctude salary and reimbursement for expenses or
per diem received fron a state, Iocal, or federal government
agency. (Section 82030 (b) (2).
Councilmernber Price's spouse also receives income from the
district's 
firefighters' 
union as compensation for his duties as
president of the union. You have stated that this income exceeds
$ZSO i., a twelve month peri-od. For purposes of our analysis we
assume that this incone exceeds $5oo in a tlrelve month period,
$250 of which would be attributed to Councilmember Price as her

Governmental Decision

judgment on the part of the official 
or

agency. Attempts to influence include, but are not J,inited to,
appearances or contacts by the official 
0n behal-f of a business
entity, client, or custoner. (Regulation 1g7o0.1.)
Accordingl-y, if it is reasonably foreseeabfe that
Councilmember pricers economic interlst in the income of her
spouse wil] be rnateriarly- affected by a governmental decision, she
must not only disqualify herself from paiticipating in forrnal
decisions of the city councir whi.ch rnay artecl =ucfr int"r"st, 
but
she nust also abstain from attempting Lo influence such deci-sions

by communicating with other members of the city council or the
staff regarding the decisions.
Foreseeabilitv
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial ]ikelihood that it $rill olcur. 
To be
foreseeabLe, the effect of a decision nust be nore than a mere
possibility; 
however, certainty is not required.

The City of Antioch is considering the reorganizatj.on of the
Riverview Fire protection District. 
the districi.,s firefighters,
union is a source of incorne to councilmernber price. 
you s€ate in
your request for advice that the proposed reorganization of the
district 
could lead to a change in the conduct of labor relations
within the district 
in that, for example, instead of the county
Board of Supervisors making decisions on labor relations issues, a
locally-elected fire district 
board or the city councj.I could
deterrnine those j-ssues in the future. 
If it i; reasonably
foreseeable that the reorganization wirl affect the incorne which
the spouse of Councilrnenber price receives from the union by $250
or more j,n a twefve month period (Regulation 18702.1(a) (4)), or
that the reorganization will- have a material financial effect on
the union, CounciLmember price,s disqual i f ication would be
requ j-red.
Mater ia l itv
Regulation t-8702 sets forth the guidelines for detennining
!'rhether an official,s 
economic interest in a decision is
materially affected by a decision. If the official,s 
econonic
interest is directLv involved in the decision, Regulation ta702.l
provides the appropriate standard for assessing rniteriality.
However, when the official,s 
economic interests wilL be afiected
indirectlv by a decision, the appropriate standards for
determining materiality.are those of ReguLations 18702.2 through
LA102.6. These regulations are discussed beIow.
A person or business entity is directly involved in a
decision before an official/s 
agency when that person or entity,
either personally or by an agent:
(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the
decision will be made by filing an application,
claim, appeal, or similar request or,.

of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official 
or the official's 
agency.
(3) A person or business entity j-s the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issiance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
of any license, permit, or other entitlenent to, or
contrict with, the subiect person or business
entity.
Regulation 18702 ' 1(b) .

union has initiated
the proposed reorglnization of the fire district 
or that it is the

union indirectly'

18703.) For purposes of o,]r discussion, the public is the entire

bwln (1976) 2 FPPC ops. 77; Jorqensen Advice Letter, No' A-90-
017. )
Theeffectofdecisionsregardingthereorganj'zationofthe
f i-re district 
on Councilrnernber irice or on her source of i-ncome is
distinguishabLe from the effect of the decisions on the population

of the City of Antioch and thus the "public generally" exception
does not appfy. This is so because the econornic interests of the
residents of the city witl not be affected in the sane manner as
those of Dembers of the union and j.n particular those of Mr- Price

The district also provides services to the City of Pittsburg and to the unincorporated communj,ty of west Pittsburg- The district is governed by the contra costa county Board of Supervisors. A locaL appointed fire commission rnakes re^cornmendations to the Board of Supervisors. The City of Antioch is considering a number of ideas about the district's future organization.
